pkg/kafka.purpose: Shared low-level Kafka utilities: typed config structs that produce kafka.ConfigMap, a TopicProvisioner with LRU cache, log bridging from librdkafka to slog, and an OTel metrics sub-package. Primary constraint: all code here is infrastructure plumbing — no domain or business logic.

patterns.name: ConfigMapper + ConfigValidator interfaces
patterns.description: Every config struct (CommonConfigParams, ConsumerConfigParams, ProducerConfigParams) implements both ConfigMapper (AsConfigMap() → kafka.ConfigMap) and ConfigValidator (Validate() error). Composite configs (ConsumerConfig, ProducerConfig) embed the parts and delegate to mergeConfigsToMap + sequential validators.
patterns.example: type ConsumerConfig struct { CommonConfigParams; ConsumerConfigParams }; func (c ConsumerConfig) AsConfigMap() (kafka.ConfigMap, error) { return mergeConfigsToMap(c.CommonConfigParams, c.ConsumerConfigParams) }

patterns.name: configValue interface for enum types
patterns.description: Every string-based enum (BrokerAddressFamily, Partitioner, AutoOffsetReset, TimeDurationMilliSeconds) implements fmt.Stringer + encoding.TextUnmarshaler + json.Unmarshaler. Validation uses a ValidValues[T] slice with AsKeyMap() for O(1) lookup.

patterns.name: TopicProvisioner LRU cache + noop guard
patterns.description: NewTopicProvisioner returns a TopicProvisionerNoop when no Kafka admin is available. The concrete topicProvisioner skips already-cached topics in Provision and skips protected topics in DeProvision. Always treat ErrTopicAlreadyExists and ErrUnknownTopicOrPart as success, not errors.

patterns.name: Sequential error returns in constructors
patterns.description: NewTopicProvisioner validates each required field (AdminClient, Logger, Meter) with explicit nil checks before creating metrics, returning immediately on first failure. OTel metric registration errors are also returned sequentially, not collected.
patterns.example: if config.AdminClient == nil { return nil, errors.New("kafka admin client is required") }

patterns.name: LogProcessor oklog/run-compatible signature
patterns.description: LogProcessor returns (execute func() error, interrupt func(error)) — the oklog/run pair. It starts an internal context for cancellation. ConsumeLogChannel is a simpler goroutine-targeted variant that ranges over the log channel directly.
patterns.example: func LogProcessor(logEmitter LogEmitter, logger *slog.Logger) (execute func() error, interrupt func(error))

patterns.name: TimeDurationMilliSeconds rendered as millisecond integer string
patterns.description: TimeDurationMilliSeconds.String() returns the duration in milliseconds as a decimal string (e.g. "6000" for 6s). This is the format librdkafka expects for *.ms config keys. Never pass raw time.Duration to kafka.ConfigMap.

key_file_guides.role: Defines all Kafka config param structs and their ConfigMapper/ConfigValidator implementations. mergeConfigsToMap merges multiple ConfigMapper outputs into one kafka.ConfigMap.
key_file_guides.watch_for: metadata.max.age.ms is always set to 3× TopicMetadataRefreshInterval — do not set it independently. localhost/127.0.0.1 brokers auto-force BrokerAddressFamilyIPv4 if BrokerAddressFamily is empty.

key_file_guides.role: TopicProvisioner interface + LRU-cached implementation + TopicProvisionerNoop. Owns topic creation/deletion against Kafka admin API with metrics.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ProtectedTopics prevents accidental deletion — always populate this list with system topics. Cache is expirable LRU; cache eviction fires a metrics counter via evictCallback, which must use a short-lived context, not the caller's ctx.

key_file_guides.role: Bridges librdkafka syslog-level integers to slog.Level. Requires go.logs.channel.enable=true in kafka.ConfigMap (set automatically by ConsumerConfigParams and ProducerConfigParams).
key_file_guides.watch_for: go.logs.channel.enable and go.application.rebalance.enable are hardcoded in ConsumerConfigParams.AsConfigMap() — do not remove them or librdkafka logs will silently drop.

key_file_guides.role: Package doc only — intentionally empty aside from the package declaration.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No init() or global state here; keep it that way.

key_file_guides.role: OTel Int64Gauge metrics translated from librdkafka JSON stats snapshots. Separate sub-package so callers opt in.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All metrics are gauges (point-in-time snapshots), never counters/histograms. Nil guard required at top of Add because librdkafka may omit stats sections.

anti_patterns: Setting kafka.ConfigMap keys directly instead of going through CommonConfigParams.AsConfigMap / mergeConfigsToMap — bypasses validation and the localhost IPv4 auto-fix.
Using Int64Counter or Histogram for librdkafka-derived stats — they are rolling-window snapshots, not cumulative deltas; only Int64Gauge is correct.
Passing raw time.Duration as a kafka.ConfigMap value for *.ms keys — must use TimeDurationMilliSeconds which renders as millisecond integer string.
Accessing stats sections in metrics.Add without a nil guard — librdkafka JSON can omit broker/topic/consumer-group sections entirely.
Mixing cooperative and eager PartitionAssignmentStrategy values — ConsumerConfigParams.Validate() rejects this combination explicitly.

decisions.decision: ConfigMapper + ConfigValidator split into separate interfaces implemented by value receivers
decisions.rationale: Composite configs (ConsumerConfig, ProducerConfig) embed multiple param structs; mergeConfigsToMap iterates them independently so each part owns its own key namespace and validation without coordinating with others.

decisions.decision: TopicProvisioner uses an expirable LRU cache keyed by topic name
decisions.rationale: Provision is called on every namespace creation; the cache prevents redundant Kafka admin round-trips for topics that already exist, while TTL-based expiry avoids stale cache entries after broker-side topic deletion.

decisions.decision: OTel metrics live in a separate metrics/ sub-package behind functional options
decisions.rationale: Not all consumers of pkg/kafka need stats instrumentation; separating it avoids pulling OTel metric dependencies into the base config package and lets callers opt in per sub-metric category.

code_examples.scenario: Constructing and validating a ConsumerConfig then provisioning topics
